title: Tech-Stocks Retreat Deepens Ahead of US Payrolls: Markets Wrap
summary: The latest bout of volatility in technology stocks entered a second day, with chipmakers in South Korea bearing the brunt of the selling and declines following through to Europe’s tech sector and the Nasdaq 100. The dollar fell and Treasuries wavered ahead of the June US payrolls report.

title: Leveraged ETFs Are Just the Tip of the Iceberg: MacroScope
summary: Record leverage is sweeping through the financial system, exposing hedge funds, banks, retail traders and money market funds to potential mounting losses.

title: Gold Extends Gains After Warsh Comments Ease Rate-Hike Prospects
summary: Gold extended a rebound after remarks by US Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh damped speculation the central bank may hike interest rates this year to tackle inflation.

title: JPMorgan Joins Gulf Banks on $7 Billion Loan for Syrian Projects
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is joining Gulf lenders to help arrange about $7 billion of debt financing for a Qatari firm’s projects in Syria, marking one of the biggest foreign commitments to the country’s reconstruction since dictator Bashar al-Assad was ousted by rebel forces.

title: Tech Stocks Fall Ahead of US Payrolls; Apple, OpenAI Bid with Government | Bloomberg Brief 7/2/2026
summary: Technology stocks fall ahead of the US June payrolls report. The yen strengthens sharply against the dollar as traders stay alert for a potential intervention from the Japanese government. Apple is said to have appealed to the Trump administration in an effort to buy Chinese-made memory chips from firms on Pentagon's blacklist. OpenAI is reportedly having discussions about giving the US government a 5% stake in the company. Veronica Clark of Citi looks ahead to the June jobs report. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Saudi Supertankers Exit Hormuz in Kingdom’s Biggest Oil Flow Since Iran War Truce
summary: Saudi Arabia is exporting the most crude from inside the Persian Gulf since the Iran war blocked the Strait of Hormuz, as producers across the region boost shipments following an interim peace deal between Washington and Tehran.

title: Yen Climbs With Traders on High Alert for Intervention Risks
summary: The yen strengthened sharply against the dollar amid rising speculation that the currency’s continued weakness may prompt a fresh round of intervention by Japan.

title: Investors Are Using Leveraged ETFs to Turbocharge AI Bets
summary: Funds have begun to magnify swings in stocks like SK Hynix, analysts say

title: US Jobs Preview: What to Watch for in the June Report
summary: US employment growth is expected to show an improvement in June with the unemployment rate holding steady at 4.3%. Michael McKee has a preview on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: French Heat Wave Kills Poultry, Corn Crops in Key Farming Nation
summary: France’s record-breaking heat has cut as much as 30% of corn production in Europe’s top farming nation and killed hundreds of thousands of poultry.

title: Treasuries Slip Before Payrolls as Traders Weigh Warsh Comments
summary: Treasuries edged lower as traders awaited a US employment print that may give clues on the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate path after Chairman Kevin Warsh struck a dovish tone on inflation.

title: China and US Target Tariff Reductions on Agricultural Products
summary: China and the US are seeking to roll back tariffs on some agricultural products, as they look to preserve a broader trade truce that was struck last year.

title: Meta Fuels AI Capacity Glut Fears, Chip Stocks Slump | The Pulse 7/2/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Paul Donovan, UBS Global Wealth Management Chief Economist, Vlad Tenev, Robinhood CEO, Jim DeMare, Bank of America Co-President, George Arison, Grindr CEO  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Brazil Rail Operator Opens New Route to Boost Soymeal Exports
summary: Brazilian railway operator VLI SA is starting a new export route for soybean meal, addressing key bottlenecks as the country attempts to boost shipments of the animal feed ingredient.

title: Tankmaker KNDS Delays IPO as Investors Cool on Defense Stocks
summary: Tankmaker KNDS postponed its initial public offering, as weakness in European defense stocks derailed what was expected to be the biggest first-time share sale in Europe this summer.

title: Oil Extends Slide as More Barrels Flow Through Strait of Hormuz
summary: Oil extended its slide to pre-war levels as flows through the Strait of Hormuz increase and traders grapple with signs of oversupply.

title: Morgan Stanley Among Banks Challenging Growing Dollar Optimism
summary: Currency strategists at Credit Agricole, Morgan Stanley and TD Securities are among those bucking the consensus call for a stronger dollar.

title: Prasad: Oil Flow is Up But Market Uncertainty Remains
summary: Oil extended its drop for a third day, as flows through the Strait of Hormuz climbed. Aathira Prasad, Director of Macroeconomics at Nasser Saidi & Associates, spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the oil supply and prices for the rest of 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Yen's Sharp Gain Unlikely a Market Movement, Natixis Says
summary: Alicia Garcia Herrero, chief APAC economist at Natixis, says that Japan may already have intervened in currency markets after the yen strengthened sharply against the dollar on Thursday. "Nobody said so, but I would argue that that correction can't happen if everybody's reading your screens thinking that US data will be strong," Garcia Herrero tells Bloomberg Television. The abrupt move came as traders await US jobs figures later Thursday, which have the potential to move the dollar-yen currency pair. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Quantum Systems More Than Doubles Valuation to $8 Billion
summary: Quantum Systems has raised $1.2 billion in a funding round, more than doubling the German drone startup’s valuation to about $8 billion post-money, in the latest example of investors’ enthusiasm for defense-related bets.

title: Hayfin Defies Private Credit Angst with €15 Billion Fund
summary: Hayfin Capital Management has raised more than €15 billion ($17.1 billion) for its new flagship direct lending fund in the latest sign that institutional investors are concentrating commitments with the biggest firms in the $1.8 trillion private credit market.

title: SBI Funds’ $1.2 Billion India IPO Launch in July to Test Demand
summary: SBI Funds Management Ltd. is set to launch its initial public offering, which could raise up to $1.2 billion, in the week starting July 13, according to people familiar with the matter, testing investor demand ahead of a busy pipeline of billion-dollar listings in India.

title: Japan Retail Investors Bought Record Stocks Last Week in Selloff
summary: Japan’s retail investors purchased a record amount of local shares last week, when a tech selloff sent equity gauges plunging.

title: Chip Stocks Slump Across Asia as Global Tech Selloff Intensifies
summary: Asian semiconductor stocks tumbled on Thursday, tracking tech losses on Wall Street after Meta Platforms Inc.’s plan to develop a business that would sell access to AI computing power raised worries about overcapacity.

title: Korean Stocks Tumble 8% as AI Jitters Trigger Chip Selloff
summary: South Korean stocks were hit by renewed doubts over the sustainability over the AI buildout boom, which drove a selloff in chipmakers that have so far underpinned the market’s world-beating performance.

title: Switzerland’s Inflation Slows for First Time in Eight Months
summary: Swiss inflation slowed in June for the first time in eight months, suggesting the impact of lower oil costs are feeding through to the domestic economy.

title: Iran’s Floating Oil Hoard Swells as Major Buyers Stay Away
summary: A hoard of Iranian oil is building up at sea, as the Islamic Republic struggles to find buyers before the expiry of a 60-day window granted by Washington.
